name: Traditional Lasagne
recipeIngredient: 2/3 Recipe Egg Pasta or use 2 eggs and 1 1/3 cups flour
6 Fresh Italian plum tomatoes (or 1 cup canned)
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the Egg Pasta dough by rolling it as thin as possible and cutting into 2-inch wide strips or approximately 4-inch squares.
2. Grate the Parmesan cheese.
3. For the Bolognese Sauce, dice the onion, carrot, and celery. Mince the garlic. Melt butter in a saucepan over medium heat; add the pork and cook until it just loses its pink color, about 3 minutes.
4. Add onion, carrot, and celery; cook until tender, about 5 minutes. Add garlic; cook for 1 minute.
5. Add wine, stirring with a wooden spoon to deglaze the pan; simmer until reduced by half, about 15 minutes.
6. Add heavy cream and nutmeg; simmer until thickened, about 5 minutes. Stir in tomatoes and their juice, breaking up with a wooden spoon. Cover and simmer over low heat, stirring occasionally, for 2 hours. Season with salt and pepper.
7. For the Bechamel Sauce, melt butter on low heat in a saucepan. Whisk in the flour and cook for 1 minute. Gradually whisk in the milk and bring to a boil, stirring constantly. Reduce heat; season with salt and pepper. Simmer for 20 minutes.
8. Cook the pasta in a large pot of boiling, salted water until tender, about 3 minutes for fresh pasta. Drain and refresh under cold water.
9. Pour a thin layer of Bolognese Sauce into the bottom of a baking dish. Arrange a layer of pasta on top. Pour 1/3 of the Bolognese sauce and then 1/3 of the Bechamel over the pasta. Sprinkle 1/4 cup Parmesan cheese.
10. Repeat for 2 more layers, ending with the Parmesan cheese.
11. NOTE: Lasagne can be made to this point up to a day ahead OR can be frozen.
12. Cooking and Serving: Heat oven to 400F. Loosely cover lasagne with foil and bake until warmed through, about 20 minutes. Remove foil and put lasagne under broiler until golden brown, about 5 minutes. Let lasagne sit for 10 minutes before serving.
Makes 4 servings.
